name: render_shader_scene_scale

symptoms: Process dies with SIGSEGV (exit 139) at the FIRST human-render camera capture — no Python traceback
Physics-only stages before the first render (scene build, contact checks, occupancy grids) all complete normally
Crash is deterministic: same location on every retry, unaffected by GPU cooldown or process isolation
trace_evidence: Kernel log: 'segfault ... in libEGL_nvidia.so' — driver-level crash, not catchable in Python
Scene is room-scale (ReplicaCAD apartment, ~478 actors) and shader_pack is a ray-tracing variant (rt / rt-fast)
Same script renders fine on small tabletop scenes with the same shader, and on the big scene with 'minimal'

when_to_apply: Any headless render that segfaults at the first frame on a large (hundreds of actors) scene while ray-tracing shader packs are enabled, especially on <=8GB GPUs. Also apply when a previously-working script starts segfaulting after a merge — check whether the merge changed the shader pack.
repair_strategy: Use the rasterizer ('minimal' / 'default') shader pack for room-scale scenes; reserve rt/rt-fast for small tabletop showcases. Make the shader an env-var override (not a hardcoded constant) so merges cannot silently re-enable ray tracing, and log the chosen shader at startup so the failure is diagnosable from the run log alone.
code_sketch: RENDER_SHADER_PACK = 'minimal'  # rt-fast segfaults in libEGL_nvidia on ~478-actor scenes
env = make_env(shader_pack=shader)  # never hardcode rt-* at call sites
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro HAB demo (ReplicaCAD apt_5, ManiSkill 3.0.1, 8GB GPU) 2026-07-02: rt-fast crashed at first capture twice in a row; switching to 'minimal' passed the same capture and the full base-drive check on the next run
